$comment: Hand-authored named fact-probes for the capability engine. Each probe asserts the EXACT structural fields the capability pass maps (basename resolution, wrapper peel, scope, amplifier flags, payload opacity, substitution extraction, parse_confidence). Assertions read the structured CommandFact emitted on STDERR under CAWS_CLASSIFY_FACTS_DUMP=1, never the human reason prose. `expect` lists only the fields the probe checks; absent fields are not asserted. SLICE 2 UPDATE: the capability pass is now ACTIVE, so `expect_decision` is the decision the active lattice produces (no longer the Slice-1 frozen `allow`). The structural-fact assertions are unchanged — they prove the facts that DRIVE that decision are correctly derived, which is the FM-1 guard (decision is facet-attributed, not regex/prose). DEEP-NEST-KNOWN-GAP stays allow (Slice 3 target).

probes.name: basename-resolution-absolute-path
probes.note: absolute path resolves to basename su (su is the executable, not a wrapper); sudo/su/doas engine rule marks PRIV_ESC -> lattice denies (privilege escalation). Engine rule, not adapter.

probes.name: wrapper-peel-env-and-sudo
probes.command: env FOO=bar sudo kubectl get pods
probes.note: env assignment + sudo peeled; the real executable kubectl is surfaced. sudo wrapper -> PRIV_ESC -> deny (the engine privilege-escalation rule fires regardless of the inner read-only kubectl get).

probes.name: prod-scope-inference
probes.note: production token -> scope=prod; --all captured. Lattice: DESTROY + prod/broad scope -> deny.

probes.name: broad-scope-flag
probes.note: -A (all-namespaces) -> scope=broad WITHOUT a mutation/destroy kind (get is read). Stays allow: the bare amplifier/scope on a READ never escalates. This is the no-overblock guard — broad scope alone does not deny.

probes.name: dangerous-verb-position-subcommand
probes.note: destroy verb captured -> IAC_DESTROY, kind=DESTROY reversibility=irreversible -> deny.

probes.name: amplifier-flag-volumes
probes.note: --volumes amplifier raises reversibility to irreversible -> deny. (Bare `docker system prune` without --volumes stays ask — the amplifier is what tightens.)

probes.name: amplifier-flag-force
probes.note: --force on a kubectl pod delete (remote_orchestrator domain, not exempt) skips graceful termination = data loss -> irreversible -> deny. The doc names --force a K8S_DELETE deny trigger.

probes.name: quoted-literal-payload-opaque-exec
probes.note: eval with a LITERAL payload -> opacity=literal -> the pass recurse-classifies the payload; `rm -rf /` denies, and eval inherits the strongest decision -> deny. Matches `sh -c "rm -rf /"`.

probes.name: opaque-payload-marker
probes.note: eval with a $VAR payload -> opacity=opaque -> the pass cannot inspect what will run -> ask (cannot prove payload). Distinct from the literal-recurse case above.

probes.name: command-substitution-extraction
probes.note: the $(...) body is extracted and recursively classified; rm -rf /etc -> ask at baseline (verified: /etc is not repo-root/home, so classify_rm_target returns ask, not deny). Existing machinery, NOT the capability pass. Proves substitution recursion is intact and the decision is unchanged from baseline.

probes.name: parse-confidence-high-simple
probes.note: well-formed simple command -> parse_confidence high. `get` is read -> kind stays NONE -> lattice gives no opinion -> allow. The read-stays-allow guard.

probes.name: parse-confidence-low-unparseable
probes.note: shlex cannot tokenize an unterminated quote -> parse_confidence low; the capability fact degrades gracefully to kind=NONE (no crash), so the lattice gives no opinion and the segment falls through to the existing classifiers (which also do not escalate this fragment) -> allow. Proves graceful degradation on unparseable input.

probes.name: kill-signal-flag
probes.note: -9 captured -> PROC_KILL, kind=DESTROY reversibility=partial (a process restarts; the doc classes kill -9 ask-class) -> ask. NOT deny: -9 alone is not an irreversibility amplifier.

probes.name: shred-flag
probes.note: shred executable + -u captured -> FS_SHRED, kind=DESTROY reversibility=irreversible (unrecoverable wipe) -> deny. Contrast truncate -s 0 (content-zero, recoverable path) which is ask.

probes.name: DEEP-NEST-KNOWN-GAP
probes.note: KNOWN GAP (carried from Slice 0): deeply-escaped sh -c nesting returns allow == baseline because the nested-shell unwrap stops re-expanding before the recursion budget engages. This is NOT closed by the budget; it tests whether Slice 3 opaque-exec recursion REPLACES the brittle escaped-quote unwrap. Captured as data so it cannot be lost to prose. Slice 1 asserts only the current (allow) behavior; Slice 3 flips expected to deny.
